title: Dates With Caramelised Onions & Honey Mascarpone
summary: Dates. Caramelised onions. Honey mascarpone. A bundle of flavours that, when brought together, make something delicious. The perfect recipe for Ramadan.

ingredients.label: Mascarpone (Or Any Type of Cream Cheese That's Not Too Sweet)
ingredients.name: Mascarpone

ingredients.label: 1 Tbsp Honey (I Used Light & Dark Honey)
ingredients.name: Honey

method.description: First, slice the onion thinly. Then, place a pan over a medium heat and add the ghee.
Immediately add ½ tbsp of honey to the ghee. I used a light flowery one for this.

Add the onions and lower the heat. You're not sautéing the onions! You want them to cook slowly for them to caramelise. If it looks like they're getting crisp, pour some water on the onions — this helps soften them.
Once slightly browned and soft, add the remaining honey.
When a jammy texture is achieved, remove from the heat. Taste and adjust sweetness accordingly. Set aside to cool. While the onions are cooling, slice and pit the dates.
Once pitted, add a smear of mascarpone to one half of the inside of the date. Scoop some onions and fill in the other half.
Serve immediately and enjoy! This can be stored in the fridge for about 3-4 days.

chefs.name: Ayesha Erkin
chefs.summary: Ayesha Erkin is a trained architect that found herself spending more late nights in the kitchen than in the studio. Her favorite type of recipe development is obsessing over one thing and making it in a 100 different ways, which resulted in her debut cookbook, 'Date Of The Day'. Ayesha is an immigrant of mixed Turkic and Arab heritage. She was born and raised in Pakistan, alongside living between Saudi Arabia, Germany and moved to America when she was 14. Ayesha is the founder of 'Brown Girls Food Club' and 'People, Places & Spaces' - a design, culinary and research practice. She is based between London and Texas, but you'll often find her traveling elsewhere.
